title: Cassandra Read and Write Latency P99 by Keyspace
dataSources: `nodetool cfstats` / `nodetool tablestats` per table ingested as `cassandra:nodetool_cfstats`
app: Scripted nodetool collector on each node; optional JMX `ClientRequest` latency metrics via TA-JMX
spl:
index=database sourcetype="cassandra:nodetool_cfstats"
| eval r99=tonumber(Percentile_99th_read_latency_ms)
| eval w99=tonumber(Percentile_99th_write_latency_ms)
| where r99 > 50 OR w99 > 50
| stats max(r99) as read_p99_ms max(w99) as write_p99_ms by keyspace_name, table_name, host
| sort -read_p99_ms
description: Table-level 99th percentile latencies isolate hot partitions and compaction debt before mean cluster metrics move. Keyspace-level rollups help tenant owners understand SLA risk on shared rings.
value: Prevents customer-visible timeouts by steering tuning (compaction, caching, consistency level) to the right tables.
implementation: Normalize nodetool output: map `Keyspace`, `Table`, and latency percentiles to consistent field names (`keyspace_name`, `table_name`, `Percentile_99th_read_latency_ms`). Poll every 5 minutes. If your C* version prints microseconds, convert in `props.conf` `EVAL`. Join with application release calendar for correlation.
visualization: Heatmap (keyspace × host for read_p99_ms), Line chart trends.
